content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

You can do the following operations:


If NNN is even, choose to change NNN to either (3⊕N)+1(3 \oplus N) + 1(3⊕N)+1 or N2\dfrac{N}{2}2N​.
If NNN is odd, change NNN to (3⊕N)+1(3 \oplus N) + 1(3⊕N)+1.
Here, ⊕\oplus⊕ denotes the Bitwise XOR operator.

Check if it is possible to go from NNN to 111, and further find the minimum operations to do so. If it is not possible, print −1-1−1.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the minimum operations for NNN to be converted to 111, or −1-1−1 if impossible.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : NNN is already 111, thus we need 000 operations.
Test Case 2 : N=2N = 2N=2 is even, and we choose to change it to N2\frac{N}{2}2N​ to get 111 in one operation.
Test Case 3 : N=3N = 3N=3 is odd, and we are forced to change NNN to (3⊕N+1)=1(3 \oplus N + 1) = 1(3⊕N+1)=1, thus only one operation needed.
